title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds bounded snapshot navigation history from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded snapshot navigation history while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go History Semant…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9321 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
